heading: Flying officer rating: qualifications
label: 9253 Flying officer rating: qualifications
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)869110:291c.10:291c–1.10:291e.(8th, 9th, and 11th provisos); added (4th sentence, less 2d proviso), ; , ; (last proviso), .June 3, 1916, ch. 134, § 13aJuly 2, 1926, ch. 721, § 244 Stat. 781June 16, 1936, ch. 587, § 349 Stat. 1524Oct. 4, 1940, ch. 74254 Stat. 963(2d proviso under “Finance Department”), .June 24, 1948, ch. 63262 Stat. 65010:291c (proviso) and the words “after ”, in 10:291c–1, are omitted as executed. The definition of the term “flying officer”, in 10:291c, originally was a definition of the term “flying officer in time of peace” as provided by , . , , eliminated the words “in time of peace”. As a consequence of that amendment, 10:291e (1st 26 words) is omitted as surplusage. Clause (2) is substituted for 10:291c–1 (less last 10 words). The words “commissioned officers or warrant”, in 10:291c–1, are omitted as surplusage. In clause (4), the last 19 words are substituted for the words “any other”.June 30, 1948section 2 of the Act of July 2, 1926, ch. 72144 Stat. 781Section 1 of the Act of October 4, 1940, ch. 74254 Stat. 963
Amendments2018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232section 8691 of this title

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 115–232section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this titleFeb. 1, 2019

children.text: Officers who have aeronautical ratings as pilots of service types of aircraft or as aircraft observers.
Officers who are members of combat crews, other than pilots of service types of aircraft, aircraft observers, and observers.
In time of war, officers who have aeronautical ratings as observers.
